back_translation: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:
'Avenge the vengeance of the sons of Israel from the Midianites; after that you shall be gathered to your people.'
And Moses spoke to the people, saying: 'Equip from among you men for the army, and let them be against Midian to give the vengeance of the LORD on Midian.'
'A thousand to a tribe, a thousand to a tribe, for all the tribes of Israel you shall send to the army.'
And there were delivered from the thousands of Israel, a thousand to a tribe — twelve thousand armed for war.
And Moses sent them, a thousand to a tribe to the army — them and Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest to the army — and the vessels of the sanctuary and the trumpets of the alarm in his hand.
And they warred against Midian, as the LORD commanded Moses; and they killed every male.
And the kings of Midian they killed, in addition to their slain — Evi and Rekem and Zur and Hur and Reba, the five kings of Midian; and Balaam son of Beor they killed with the sword.
And the sons of Israel took captive the women of Midian and their little ones; and all their cattle and all their flocks and all their wealth they plundered.
And all their cities in their dwelling places, and all their encampments, they burned with fire.
And they took all the spoil and all the plunder, of man and of beast.

And they brought to Moses and to Eleazar the priest and to the congregation of the sons of Israel — the captives and the plunder and the spoil — to the camp, to the plains of Moab, which are by the Jordan of Jericho.
And Moses and Eleazar the priest and all the leaders of the congregation went out to meet them, outside the camp.
And Moses was angry against the officers of the army, the commanders of thousands and the commanders of hundreds, who came from the army of the war.
And Moses said to them: 'Have you kept alive every female?'
'Behold, these were to the sons of Israel, by the word of Balaam, to commit treachery against the LORD in the matter of Peor; and the plague was on the congregation of the LORD.'
'And now, kill every male among the little ones; and every woman who has known a man by lying with a male, kill.'
'And every female child among the women who have not known the lying with a male, keep alive for yourselves.'

'And you, encamp outside the camp seven days; everyone who killed a soul and everyone who touched a slain person, you shall purify yourselves on the third day and on the seventh day — you and your captives.'
'And every garment, and every article of leather, and every work of goats' hair, and every article of wood, you shall purify.'
And Eleazar the priest said to the men of the army, who came to the war: 'This is the statute of the law which the LORD commanded Moses.'
'Only the gold and the silver, the bronze, the iron, the tin, and the lead.'

'Every thing that comes into the fire — you shall pass through the fire, and it shall be clean; but with water of purification it shall be purified; and everything that does not come into the fire — you shall pass through the water.'
'And you shall wash your clothes on the seventh day, and you shall be clean; and afterward you shall come into the camp.'
And the LORD said to Moses, saying:
'Lift up the head of the plunder of the captives, in man and in beast — you and Eleazar the priest and the heads of the fathers of the congregation.'
'And you shall divide the plunder between the takers of war, the ones going out to the army, and between all the congregation.'
'And you shall lift up a tribute to the LORD from the men of war, the ones going out to the army — one soul out of five hundred, from man and from cattle and from donkeys and from sheep.'
'From their half you shall take it, and you shall give to Eleazar the priest a contribution of the LORD.'

'And from the half of the sons of Israel you shall take one — one taken — out of fifty, from man, from cattle, from donkeys, and from sheep, from all the cattle; and you shall give them to the Levites who keep the charge of the tabernacle of the LORD.'
And Moses and Eleazar the priest did as the LORD commanded Moses.
And the plunder, the rest of the spoil which the people of the army plundered, was — sheep: six hundred thousand and seventy thousand and five thousand.
And cattle: two and seventy thousand.
And donkeys: one and sixty thousand.
And human souls, from the women who had not known lying with a male — all souls, two and thirty thousand.
And the half, the portion of those going out in the army, was — the number of sheep: three hundred thousand and thirty thousand and seven thousand and five hundred.
And the tribute to the LORD from the sheep — six hundred five and seventy.
And the cattle — six and thirty thousand; and their tribute to the LORD — two and seventy.
And donkeys — thirty thousand and five hundred; and their tribute to the LORD — one and sixty.
And human souls — sixteen thousand; and their tribute to the LORD — two and thirty souls.
And Moses gave the tribute, the contribution of the LORD, to Eleazar the priest, as the LORD commanded Moses.
And from the half of the sons of Israel, which Moses divided from the men of the army.
And the half of the congregation was, from the sheep: three hundred thousand and thirty thousand, seven thousand and five hundred.
And cattle: six and thirty thousand.
And donkeys: thirty thousand and five hundred.
And human souls: sixteen thousand.

And Moses took from the half of the sons of Israel the taken one — one out of fifty, from man and from beast; and he gave them to the Levites, the keepers of the charge of the tabernacle of the LORD, as the LORD commanded Moses.
And the officers who were over the thousands of the army approached Moses — the commanders of thousands and the commanders of hundreds.
And they said to Moses: 'Your servants have lifted up the head of the men of war who are in our hand, and not a man of us has been missing.'
'And we have brought an offering of the LORD — a man what he found, an article of gold: armlet, and bracelet, ring, earring, and necklace — to make atonement upon our souls before the LORD.'
And Moses and Eleazar the priest took the gold from them — every article of workmanship.
And all the gold of the contribution which they lifted up to the LORD was — sixteen thousand seven hundred and fifty shekels — from the commanders of thousands, and from the commanders of hundreds.
The men of the army plundered, each man for himself.
And Moses and Eleazar the priest took the gold from the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, and brought it into the Tent of Meeting — a memorial for the sons of Israel before the LORD.
